name: citevahti
description: Run unit tests on your manuscript — check every claim before you cite it.
long_description: CiteVahti is a local-first citation-integrity tool. It runs a blinded human → AI → adjudication review of the claims in your manuscript: you rate the evidence before the AI rating is revealed, decisions are gated and undoable, and every step is recorded in a hash-chained audit trail. This extension adds CiteVahti's MCP tools and the `run_claim_tests` prompt to Claude Desktop so you can run the whole review in chat — no terminal, and no Python required (the runtime is bundled). Local-first and single-user; literature lookups use PubMed, OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar and Crossref; no telemetry.

ledger_root.title: CiteVahti folder
ledger_root.description: Where CiteVahti keeps your evidence-decision ledger (a .citevahti folder is created inside). Pick or create a folder, e.g. Documents/CiteVahti.

prompts.name: run_claim_tests
prompts.description: Run the blinded claim-by-claim citation review over your manuscript.
prompts.text: Run claim tests on my manuscript using CiteVahti.

prompts.name: screen_topic
prompts.description: Layer-0 screening: turn a topic into candidate claims + nearby evidence (leads, not verdicts), then hand off to run_claim_tests.
prompts.text: Screen a topic with CiteVahti and propose candidate claims to assess.

prompts.name: check_paragraph
prompts.description: Everyday in-writing check: paste a paragraph you're drafting and see, per sentence, what's already vetted, what needs attention, and what's new. Read-only.
prompts.text: Check this paragraph with CiteVahti against the claims I've already vetted.

prompts.name: methods_statement
prompts.description: Produce the paste-ready methods text from this ledger: the blinded human-AI-adjudication workflow paragraph, the PRISMA AI-discovery disclosure, and the flow-of-evidence counts. Read-only.
prompts.text: Give me the CiteVahti methods statement and PRISMA disclosure for this review.
